user.title: Add OUPES User Account
user.description: Create a local user account. The proxy servers will be started using the configured ports.

data_description.mail: Email address used as the account username.
data_description.port: Port for device TCP broker connections (default 8896). Your NAT rules should redirect 47.252.10.9:8896 and 47.251.14.8:9504 to this port.
data_description.http_port: Port for the HTTP REST API and firmware bind/unbind intercept server (default 8897).
data_description.sibo_port: Port for the SiBo HTTPS stub server (default 8898).

abort.already_configured: A user with this email is already configured.

user.title: Choose Add Method
user.description:
How would you like to add your OUPES unit?

1. **Generate new device key**: Automatically generates a deterministic 10-digit key from your user account (matches official app behavior).
2. **Enter existing**: Setup entirely using manual details.
3. **Import from BLE**: (If available) Pick a cached device from your OUPES MEGA BLE integration.

discover_ble.title: Select Nearby Bluetooth Device
discover_ble.description: Found these active OUPES Mega devices nearby. Picking one will auto-fill its MAC Address and Device ID in the next step.

credentials.title: Register Device
credentials.description: Register a new device to this user account. If generating a new key, WiFi credentials will be sent to the device during Bluetooth pairing.

data_description.device_id: The device ID (20 hex chars). Pre-filled if a BLE device was selected.
data_description.device_key: The device key (10 hex chars). Pre-filled if a BLE device was selected.
data_description.wifi_ssid: Optional. The name of your 2.4 GHz WiFi network. Leave blank to skip WiFi provisioning.
data_description.wifi_psk: Optional. The password for your WiFi network.

prepare_pairing.title: Prepare Device for Pairing
prepare_pairing.description:
Before pairing, the device must be factory reset and put into pairing mode:

1. Press and hold the **IoT button** on the device for **5 seconds**
2. Wait until the indicator light **flashes rapidly** (factory reset complete)
3. The IoT module (Wi-Fi/BLE) will turn off — **press the IoT button once to turn it back on**
4. The device is now in pairing mode

Click **Submit** when the device is ready.

pairing.title: Pairing Device
pairing.description: Please wait while Home Assistant attempts to pair to the unit over Bluetooth. Ensure the device's Bluetooth is active.

pairing_complete.title: Pairing Complete
pairing_complete.description: Successfully paired with this device and set the new device key via Bluetooth!

reconfigure.title: Device Settings
reconfigure.description:
View or update the device credentials. The device key is shown below — save it if you need it for other integrations (e.g. the BLE integration).

To change the device's WiFi network, enter new credentials below. The device must be in Bluetooth range. Leave blank to keep the current WiFi settings.

data_description.device_key: The 10 hex character device key. Copy this to use with the BLE integration or for backup.
data_description.mac_address: Bluetooth MAC address. Required if changing WiFi credentials.
data_description.wifi_ssid: Optional. Enter a new 2.4 GHz WiFi network name to re-provision the device. Leave blank to skip.
data_description.wifi_psk: Optional. Password for the new WiFi network.

error.missing_mac_for_pairing: A MAC address is required for Bluetooth operations.
error.pairing_device_not_found: Could not find the device via Bluetooth. Make sure you are near the device, its Bluetooth is flashing, and the MAC address is correct.
error.pairing_failed: Failed to pair with the device over Bluetooth. Try factory resetting the device's WiFi/Bluetooth settings.
error.wifi_device_not_found: Could not find the device via Bluetooth to send WiFi credentials. Make sure the device is powered on and in range.
error.wifi_provision_failed: Failed to send WiFi credentials to the device. The device may not have accepted the connection. Try again.
error.wifi_ble_not_installed: The OUPES BLE integration is not installed. It is required to send WiFi credentials to the device over Bluetooth.

abort.already_configured: This device is already associated with an account.

data_description.port: Port to listen on for device TCP broker connections.
data_description.http_port: Port for the HTTP REST API and firmware bind/unbind intercept server.
data_description.sibo_port: Port for the SiBo HTTPS stub server.
data_description.validation_mode: accept_all: accept any connecting device (log-only mode). log_only: validate device_id + device_key and warn on mismatches. accept_registered: reject devices whose device_id or device_key do not match a registered user.
data_description.debug_raw_lines: Write every raw RX and TX protocol line to oupes_mega_wifi_debug.jsonl.
data_description.debug_telemetry: Write each parsed cmd=10 telemetry payload.
